rationale: Admin API shell and provider apply/reload behavior have separable ownership.
Both tasks affect public admin contracts or runtime provider lifecycle.
API-key/audit/stats endpoints and job foundation share audit semantics but can stay in separate service subgroups.
Both expose privileged admin-facing behavior.
The admin auth UI and maintenance API are mostly frontend versus backend ownership.
Full review is still required because both expose sensitive flows.
Operations and configuration UI share layout and API client contracts.
They can be separate subgroups if shared types are reconciled early.
Maintenance UI should wait for the approved backend API contract.
Dry-run, confirmation, progress, and audit feedback belong in one coherent surface.
This wave proves the single Docker first-run no-CLI product hypothesis.
Docker, docs, and smoke evidence should be reconciled together.
Final validation is a single security-focused review and evidence task.
It should run only after Docker first-run evidence exists.
